positioningSummary: The strongest public angle is not that public @grok already has private Recall access; it is that a private Recall-connected workflow lets different AI tools share durable project memory through an explicit bridge. The proof moment should show the bridge and the remembered context in the same thread.
safeClaims: Recall can act as a shared memory layer for MCP-compatible tools and local workflows.
A private Recall-connected session can reuse context created across Grok, Claude, ChatGPT, and local model workflows when those interactions are written into Recall.
Specialists improve through versioned prompts, retrieval recipes, source runs, replay evidence, and promoted lessons rather than hidden model training.

riskFlags.issue: The draft implies public @grok on X can directly access private Recall. Current workflow requires a private Recall-connected session or an explicit bridge.
The draft says every model becomes self-improving, but the implemented loop improves specialist bundles through evidence and promotion, not model weights.
The draft is philosophical but does not give the audience a one-post proof moment they can inspect.

revisedCopy: I am building Recall as my AI memory layer, not just another chatbot memory feature. The honest demo: I start an idea in one model, write the useful context into Recall, then have a Recall-connected specialist continue from that memory with a visible source run, replay, and version change. Public @grok does not magically read my private Recall yet; the bridge is explicit. That is the point: memory becomes infrastructure you can inspect.
proofMoment: Post one short before/after: the original draft overclaims public @grok access, then the Recall-connected marketing specialist flags the automation gap, rewrites the claim, and links the source run plus replay evidence.
